achievements: Reduced document processing time by 80%, from 8-12 hours to 1.5-2 hours
Improved data extraction accuracy to 95%, virtually eliminating manual rework
Platform processes documents from over 500 institutions simultaneously without latency
Delivered over $800,000 in annual labor cost savings
Achieved 180% ROI within 14 months of launch
User satisfaction scores increased by over 40%
Ensured SLA compliance even during peak academic seasons
project.description: Designed and implemented a serverless enterprise architecture using over 20 AWS services that enabled DegreeData to automate academic document processing, credential verification, and transcript evaluation. The platform transformed document-heavy workflows into a scalable, automated, and intelligent system serving over 2,500 colleges, universities, and professional organizations globally.
project.title: Enterprise Educational Data Platform
project.technicalApproach: Built serverless compute layer with AWS Lambda and AWS Fargate orchestrated through Step Functions for sequential and parallel workflows. Integrated Amazon Textract for structured data extraction from scanned transcripts and forms with over 95% accuracy including handwritten notes. AWS Comprehend provided NLP to classify and extract academic attributes (course titles, grades, GPAs). Amazon Bedrock powered a custom document parser and RAG model to convert unstructured data into JSON for RDS ingestion. Amazon OpenSearch enabled real-time search across millions of academic records. Amazon QuickSight provided dynamic dashboards for processing queues, data quality metrics, and SLA monitoring. Data layer used RDS MySQL for transactional data, DynamoDB for semi-structured records and metadata, S3 for document storage, and ElastiCache for institutional templates. Security via WAF, Shield, Certificate Manager, KMS, Secrets Manager. API Gateway and AppSync for real-time client access. EventBridge and SQS/SNS for async microservice communication. CI/CD with CloudFormation, CodePipeline, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy.

matchedRequirements: Provide a 100% hosted integrated data warehouse and business intelligence (BI)/analytics platform
Multi-level security systems for transmission, storage and reporting of data and end-user access
Data quality checks to ensure accuracy of data in the system
Provide information on the matching process employed for student data to ensure accuracy of student-level data combined from various sources
Reporting at student, classroom, teacher, site and District levels with aggregated metrics and drill-down to student or row-level detail by security access level
Ability to integrate or embed other BI dashboards currently designed by the District (Desired)
Provide training to identified users on how to build visualizations, reports, and dashboards and optimize performance
Provide training to identified users on how to find and correct data quality issues reported by the system
Quality online help documents and job-aids
Reports or other abilities in monitoring system performance including resources, processes, and jobs

Ability to integrate with data sources listed in Table 1 (Aeries, CAASPP, ELPAC, NWEA MAP, Illuminate, SEIS, itsLearning, Renaissance STAR, DIBELS, College Board Suite, IXL, NSC, CCGI, CSAC, Google Suite, Apex Learning, Edmentum, IB, CA Physical Fitness Test, CA Dept of Education Research Files, survey data, District Adopted Curriculum Digital Components)
Ability to provide client with scripts for data integration with AERIES
Provide updates that are easy to access and install
Provide documentation on all stored procedures and functions including purpose, parameters, and usage samples
Provide on-going technical support for development of new metrics, integration of new data sources, and performance of the system
Data lake capabilities enabling SBCUSD to securely upload, store, and manage ad hoc datasets from various sources supporting structured and unstructured data formats

Enable the District to design and manage intervention programs, provide dashboards evaluating intervention effectiveness, and support creation of individualized student plans for continuous progress monitoring
Provide resumes or profiles of key personnel assigned including Project Leader, Implementation Team, and Customer Service Key Contact

recommendations: 76 critical gap(s) identified. Consider teaming arrangements or subcontractors with relevant experience.
Overall past performance coverage is below 70%. This may significantly impact win probability.
Limited past performance examples available. Consider adding more past projects to the database.
criticalGaps: Shall be compliant with all federal, state, and SBCUSD policies, laws, and regulations
Shall include sufficient licenses for implementation District-wide (approximately 44,000 students)
Shall include all future software upgrades and enhancements
Ability to track student data including assessment, academic performance, demographics, attendance, program participation, behavior, interventions and student plans
Ability to track staff data including attendance, assignment and demographics
Easily import state mandated assessment results (e.g., CAASPP, ELPAC)
Easily import other student assessments unique to secondary schools (e.g., AP, IB)
Easily import other student assessments unique to SBCUSD (e.g., Standards-Aligned Assessments, formative assessments)
Ability to track and report National Student Clearinghouse (NSC) data to monitor postsecondary enrollment and completion outcomes
Describe if the system uses any third-party software and level of support provided
Support for both Windows and Mac
Compatible with District server hardware (HP)
Compatibility with mobile device platforms
Ability for technical staff to connect and query the data warehouse using industry-standard tools and query languages
Catastrophic event recovery contingency plan with turnaround less than 1 week
User authentication via Active Directory (Desired)
Ability to monitor and download (preferably into CSV) application usage data from end-users such as user traffic to BI views
Role-based security options giving SBCUSD maximum flexibility in assigning roles to users and administrators
Possibility of data scrambling or visualizations with made-up data for training purposes (Desired)
Report out data quality issues segregated by specific business owner groups with communication methods (e.g., push email notifications, daily BI reports)
Ability to query data and extract the data that is queried
Implement and maintain data quality checks unique to SBCUSD between two or more data elements (Desired)
Capacity for report and data exports to other formats (e.g., Excel, Access)
Reports in PDF format for printing with minimal customization from end-users
Ability to create both static and dynamic reporting
Customizable reporting tool that supports complex queries (e.g., Boolean Logic) (Desired)
Capacity to create a variety of visualization types including bar charts, line charts, Venn diagrams, scatter plots, and crosstabs
Allow District staff to generate and disseminate customized reports and data dashboards for authenticated users and for the public
Ability to show time-based or longitudinal measures
Ability to perform GIS mapping (Desired)
Ability to incorporate interactive features into dashboards (filters, highlights, dropdowns, single or multi-select)
Ability for users to download data used to create an aggregate measure into CSV format
Ability for users to generate custom groupings of students for targeted analysis, unique to end-user or shared amongst end-users
Ability to embed reports or dashboards into District website
Ability to include instructions or help documents to guide user interaction with reports and dashboards (Desired)
Allows for branding customization by the District (logos, fonts, color schemes) (Desired)
Pre-built reports or metrics readily usable by the District following data validation (Desired)
Analytics capabilities including advanced analytics such as predictive analytics and inferential statistics (Desired)
Ability to incorporate language translation of reports or dashboards
Ability to perform natural language querying allowing users to generate visualizations and reports by typing plain-language questions
Provide training on designing, building, and troubleshooting metrics compiled from the data warehouse
Vendor-supplied technical support and staff/vendor-staffed help-desk
BI system capable of embedding training materials for end users (Desired)
Vendor-provided workshops for selected staff such as training-of-trainers (Desired)
Training to end users on how to use visualizations, reports and dashboards; training materials subject to review and approval by SBCUSD
Ability to export/store data in a variety of regional databases
Capacity for the system to be hosted by District if deemed desirable by SBCUSD
Capacity for District to access the database with backend data analysis tools
Provide an implementation plan and project schedule showing critical milestones with tasks, beginning and end dates, and responsible parties
During implementation, provide weekly progress reports summarizing actions, issues, resolutions, deadlines, or tasks at risk (Desired)
Clearly define SBCUSD and Offeror responsibilities for implementation and on-going maintenance
Testing environment available to client for testing (Desired)
Email protocols or other notification features to communicate issues about system performance, health, data issues, quality issues (Desired)
Plan to address fault tolerance, high availability, replication strategy, on-site replication options, stress testing, load balancing, and estimated recovery time for multiple scenarios
Capacity for interface/integration with District Student Information System AERIES
Integration with SBCUSD's District-Adopted curricula (Desired)
Ability to integrate data sources unique to SBCUSD beyond those listed, requiring minimal conforming from existing data layouts
Nightly student roster refreshing
On-demand and real-time demographic data refreshing
Update data integration regularly to match state and national data layouts without intervention from SBCUSD
Intuitive, simple and user-friendly tools for end users
Import 5 years of historical data
Solution should support interoperability, reusable datasets, and efficient data exploration while minimizing duplicative or parallel data pipelines
Comply with FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act) and California Education Code Section 49073.1 (AB 1584)
Comply with Workers' Compensation requirements per California Labor Code Section 3700
Certification regarding Debarment, Suspension, Ineligibility and Voluntary Exclusion
Iran Contracting Act Certification per Public Contract Code Section 2204
Federal Funding Contract Compliance (EDGAR) requirements
Vendor must have been in business under the same license and company name for at least five consecutive years and have completed at least five similar projects
Maintain Commercial General Liability insurance with combined single limits not less than $1,000,000 per occurrence and $2,000,000 aggregate
Maintain Commercial Automobile Liability insurance with combined single limits not less than $1,000,000 each occurrence
Maintain Professional Errors and Omissions insurance not less than $1,000,000 per claim/$2,000,000 aggregate
Maintain Cyber Security and Privacy Liability insurance with combined single limits not less than $1,000,000 per occurrence and $2,000,000 aggregate
Maintain Workers' Compensation insurance as required under California Labor Code with Employers Liability not less than $1,000,000 per accident/injury/disease
Pricing shall be held firm for the duration of the contract except for inflationary adjustment approved by the District annually
No proposal may be withdrawn for a period of sixty (60) days after stated due date
